title: The One with the Ride Along

jokes.text: Hey, man. What's up? / Just thinking about Emily getting married tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Monica asks the group to keep Ross' mind off Emily's wedding
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately brings up the exact thing they're supposed to avoid
jokes.explanation: Classic immediate irony - the plan fails instantly. Well-structured with efficient setup/payoff.

jokes.text: Oh, Ross! Look, look! / What? Where? / Right over there! There! Look, look, look! / What am I looking at? / Somebody help me out here.
jokes.setup: Phoebe desperately tries to distract Ross by pointing at nothing
jokes.punchline: Ross can't see anything and needs help understanding the 'distraction'
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of awkward distraction attempt. Phoebe's frantic energy sells it.

jokes.text: He let me ride around with him. We saw and prevented crimes.
jokes.setup: Phoebe was out with Gary the cop
jokes.punchline: Her casual tone about 'preventing crimes' like it's everyday activity
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's innocent delivery of dramatic police work as casual fun.

jokes.text: Really? You? / Yeah. / Well, it's kind of dangerous. / Well, I like danger.
jokes.setup: Chandler wants to go on a dangerous ride-along
jokes.punchline: Chandler claiming he 'likes danger' when he's famously risk-averse
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contradiction - Chandler is notably not adventurous or brave.

jokes.text: You didn't say it was gonna be at nighttime.
jokes.setup: Chandler just claimed he likes danger
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets nervous about it being nighttime
jokes.explanation: Excellent quick reversal of Chandler's bravado. Perfect character beat.

jokes.text: I've broken them down into categories. We got holidays, birthdays, candids... And I've cross-referenced them by subject.
jokes.setup: Monica wants to organize photos
jokes.punchline: Her obsessively detailed categorization system
jokes.explanation: Classic Monica obsessive-compulsive behavior. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: So if you're looking up, say... birthdays and dogs, you get photo 152. See? Oh, it's me and LePoo.
jokes.setup: Monica demonstrates her filing system
jokes.punchline: The dog's name 'LePoo' sounds like 'le poo'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay joke with the dog name. Decent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You can also find him under 'dog' and 'dead'.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining her photo filing system
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning the dead dog is also filed under 'dead'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Monica's organizational obsession extends to categorizing death. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: Good thing you numbered them. / I hadn't. Photo 152 was a prototype.
jokes.setup: Rachel drops all the photos, sarcastically notes they're numbered
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals she hadn't actually numbered them - 152 was just an example
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - we think Monica's prepared but she's not. Rachel's sarcasm backfires.

jokes.text: Ross has the blender. / Everything's just falling apart.
jokes.setup: Photos scattered, Monica wants a drink to cope
jokes.punchline: Not having a blender becomes evidence that 'everything' is falling apart
jokes.explanation: Monica's dramatic overreaction to minor inconveniences. Good character work.

jokes.text: No. While you're at Ross', if you see any lying around. / What? / I don't do that.
jokes.setup: Monica asks Rachel to get cash from ATM
jokes.punchline: Monica casually suggests stealing cash from Ross, Rachel is horrified
jokes.explanation: Monica's casual suggestion of theft vs Rachel's moral response. Good contrast.

jokes.text: Okay, but it is officially nighttime.
jokes.setup: Chandler claimed to like danger but was worried about nighttime
jokes.punchline: Still obsessing over the danger of darkness
jokes.explanation: Good callback to his earlier nervousness. Chandler's cowardice is consistent.

jokes.text: Want me to grab the berry? / It's called a cherry. / It's called... Chandler!
jokes.setup: Joey asks about police siren light using wrong word
jokes.punchline: Correction leads to calling Chandler a 'cherry' (inexperienced cop)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay leading to mild insult. Police terminology humor.

jokes.text: You can't eat that in my car. / Even though my tax dollars paid for this car. / Your tax dollars?
jokes.setup: Gary won't let Joey eat messy sandwich in police car
jokes.punchline: Joey claims ownership via taxes, Gary questions if Joey pays taxes
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Joey's false sense of civic contribution questioned.

jokes.text: Did I say you could smell it? / I can't smell it? / Half the taste is in the smell. You're sucking up all the taste units.
jokes.setup: Joey is protective of his amazing sandwich
jokes.punchline: Joey's ridiculous theory about 'taste units' being depleted by smelling
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic - 'taste units' is wonderfully ridiculous. Very Joey-specific reasoning.

jokes.text: Okay, I'll give them back.
jokes.setup: Joey claims Chandler is stealing taste units by smelling
jokes.punchline: Chandler plays along with the absurd concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to Joey's absurd logic. Great economy of words.

jokes.text: Imagine the best sex you ever had. / Are you thinking about Monica? / Yeah. / What's that like?
jokes.setup: Joey asks Chandler to imagine best sex to describe sandwich
jokes.punchline: Chandler admits it's Monica, Joey asks what that's like
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline with character-specific awkwardness about Monica relationship.

jokes.text: Please don't be from a real dinosaur. Please, please. Please, please. / 'Made in Mexico.' Yes!
jokes.setup: Rachel finds something dinosaur-related at Ross's apartment
jokes.punchline: Her relief that it's fake, not from a real dinosaur
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Rachel's reaction to Ross's paleontology obsession.

jokes.text: Who would buy this?
jokes.setup: Rachel looking at Ross's dinosaur merchandise
jokes.punchline: Her incredulous reaction to his purchases
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about Ross's nerdy purchases. Functional but basic.

jokes.text: So all we had was ice? / See if he has ice.
jokes.setup: Monica needs margarita ingredients from Ross
jokes.punchline: They only have ice, but Monica still asks Rachel to check for ice
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic - checking if Ross has the one thing they already have. Good economy.

jokes.text: You gonna squeeze the perp's shoes a bit before he lawyers up? / He's a witness, not a perp. And no one talks like that.
jokes.setup: Joey tries to use cop slang
jokes.punchline: Gary corrects both his terminology and unrealistic TV cop speak
jokes.explanation: Joey's TV-influenced cop speak getting corrected. Good reality check.

jokes.text: Yeah. No one talks like that. / Oh, what, like you're Mr. Cop? / I'm more cop than you two.
jokes.setup: Ross agrees no one talks like TV cops
jokes.punchline: Chandler claims to be 'more cop' than the others
jokes.explanation: Chandler's competitive claim despite being the most cowardly. Character-specific.

jokes.text: I'm in the front seat, okay? I'm Gary's partner. / When you say 'partner', it doesn't sound cop. It sounds gay.
jokes.setup: Chandler claims to be Gary's partner
jokes.punchline: Joey points out the word 'partner' has romantic connotations
jokes.explanation: Wordplay about double meaning of 'partner.' Mild 90s-era humor.

jokes.text: Hey, Gary. Who am I? / Phone home.
jokes.setup: Ross doing impression for Gary
jokes.punchline: E.T. impression with finger pointing
jokes.explanation: Basic pop culture reference. Ross trying to be fun but it's corny.

jokes.text: Look at Officer Ross riding back here with the visitors. / What's up with that, Serpico?
jokes.setup: Ross got moved to back seat
jokes.punchline: Joey mocks him with movie reference to Serpico
jokes.explanation: Joey using movie reference to mock Ross. Decent character dynamic.

jokes.text: What if Ross hears that and calls her and they get back together? Is that what you want? Ross back with that controlling, neurotic, crazy Emily who wouldn't let him see you?
jokes.setup: Monica wants to erase Emily's message
jokes.punchline: Her rapid-fire list of Emily's negative qualities
jokes.explanation: Monica's protective sister instincts leading to character assassination. Good escalation.

jokes.text: Look, I'm his sister and I love him. I don't want to see him get hurt. / Doesn't that give me the right to control... / Help him?
jokes.setup: Monica justifying interfering with Ross's message
jokes.punchline: She accidentally says 'control' instead of 'help'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - her controlling nature slips out before she corrects herself.

jokes.text: Break up their marriage on their wedding day? Who would do that? / ... / Okay, fine. All right. But that was different.
jokes.setup: Monica asks who would break up a wedding
jokes.punchline: Rachel's silence reminds her that Ross literally did this
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to series history. Monica's hypocrisy exposed by uncomfortable silence.

jokes.text: Your messages have been erased. / No!
jokes.setup: Monica and Rachel debating whether to erase the message
jokes.punchline: Machine automatically erases while they're talking
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing - the machine makes the decision for them. Good dramatic irony.

jokes.text: What is it? / It says you can't sue if you scrape your knee or get your head blown off.
jokes.setup: Gary hands them a waiver to sign
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of head being blown off alongside scraping knee
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy - extreme escalation from minor to fatal injuries. Good contrast.

jokes.text: Oh, hurry up. I want to sign that.
jokes.setup: Waiver mentions getting head blown off
jokes.punchline: Joey eagerly wants to sign the dangerous waiver
jokes.explanation: Joey's enthusiasm for danger contrasts with others' fear. Good character beat.

jokes.text: What is he doing? What the hell is he doing? / What is it? / Is everything okay? / What? / He sees us. Don't move. And don't look at him!
jokes.setup: Someone approaches their car
jokes.punchline: Escalating panic from the guys, especially Chandler
jokes.explanation: Building tension comedy - their fear escalates over nothing threatening.

jokes.text: Hey, it's okay. It was just a car backfire.
jokes.setup: Everyone panicking and hitting the deck
jokes.punchline: All the drama was over a harmless car backfire
jokes.explanation: Classic anticlimax - all the fear was for nothing. Sets up Joey's heroic moment.

jokes.text: Hey, look at that. You tried to save your buddy. / You see that? You see what he did?
jokes.setup: Car backfire made everyone duck
jokes.punchline: Joey threw himself over Ross to protect him
jokes.explanation: Sets up Joey's heroic character development. Sweet moment that becomes important later.

jokes.text: Maybe that's Emily leaving the exact same message.
jokes.setup: Ross's answering machine is blinking again
jokes.punchline: Monica hopes it's the same message they just erased
jokes.explanation: Monica's desperate hope for an easy solution to their problem.

jokes.text: Hey, Ross, it's you. I just want you to remember this feeling. You are lucky to be alive. So live every day to the fullest. Love yourself, okay? And also get stamps. Bye.
jokes.setup: Dramatic near-death experience message
jokes.punchline: Mundane reminder to get stamps at the end
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anticlimax - profound life advice followed by mundane errand. Perfect comedy structure.

jokes.text: Play that message for Emily, and this problem goes away.
jokes.setup: Ross left himself a philosophical message about living fully
jokes.punchline: Monica thinks this will solve their Emily problem
jokes.explanation: Monica's scheme to use Ross's own message against Emily. Clever manipulation.

jokes.text: Anybody want to meet a hero? / John Glenn is here? / No. Joey.
jokes.setup: Chandler announces a hero is here
jokes.punchline: Monica expects famous astronaut, gets Joey instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up expectation of real hero, subverts with Joey.

jokes.text: It was a car backfire. / But he didn't know that. / Yeah, I didn't know that. / It could just as easily have been a bullet.
jokes.setup: Gary explains it wasn't real danger
jokes.punchline: Joey and Chandler defend the heroic act despite no real danger
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Joey's heroism stands regardless of actual danger level.

jokes.text: Ever think about joining the force? We could use a guy like you. / Who jumps at loud noises.
jokes.setup: Gary seems to offer Joey a job as police
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out Joey just jumps at noises
jokes.explanation: Chandler deflating Joey's heroic moment with practical observation.

jokes.text: If the car that backfired had run over you!
jokes.setup: Joey says he could have died tonight
jokes.punchline: Chandler's ridiculous scenario of death by the same car
jokes.explanation: Chandler's sarcastic escalation of Joey's dramatic claims. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: You know what? If I heard a shot right now, I'd throw my body on you. / Maybe we should walk in a bad neighborhood.
jokes.setup: Joey declares he'd protect Chandler
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggests deliberately finding danger to test this
jokes.explanation: Chandler's dark humor about testing Joey's protection offer.

jokes.text: I'd hate to save your life and kick your ass on the same day.
jokes.setup: Joey being protective and tough
jokes.punchline: Threatening to beat up the person he just saved
jokes.explanation: Joey's contradictory tough guy persona - save and threaten same person.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. You hate Ross! / I don't hate Ross. / Of course, you do. I saved him. You're mad at me. It adds up.
jokes.setup: Chandler notices Joey is upset
jokes.punchline: Chandler's completely wrong conclusion about Joey hating Ross
jokes.explanation: Chandler's analytical mind reaches wrong conclusion. Good character logic.

jokes.text: You know? I mean, Ross needs help. He's not 'street' like us.
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining why he saved Ross
jokes.punchline: Chandler calling himself and Joey 'street' when they're not
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Chandler thinking he's street-smart is hilarious given his privileged background.

jokes.text: All right, look. I wasn't trying to save Ross. Okay? My sandwich was next to Ross. All right? I was trying to save my sandwich.
jokes.setup: Joey seemed heroic saving Ross from gunfire
jokes.punchline: He was actually trying to save his sandwich
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - completely reframes Joey's heroic act. Perfect character-specific motivation that's both funny and believable.

jokes.text: From a bullet? / I know it doesn't make much sense. / 'Much' sense?
jokes.setup: Joey admits he tried to save sandwich from bullet
jokes.punchline: Chandler's disbelief at the understatement 'much sense'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurdity. Chandler's incredulity is perfect.

jokes.text: Look, it was instinct! I just went for it. / You risked your life for a sandwich? / I know it sounds crazy... but this is the greatest sandwich in the world!
jokes.setup: Joey defending his sandwich-saving instincts
jokes.punchline: His passionate defense of the sandwich's worth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - food is worth risking life for. Builds on sandwich obsession established earlier.

jokes.text: So you didn't choose Ross before me? / No. I would never do that. You're like my brother.
jokes.setup: Chandler worried Joey chose Ross over him
jokes.punchline: Joey's sweet declaration that Chandler is like a brother
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment. Resolves the conflict with genuine emotion.

jokes.text: Here. / Thanks. / Easy, it's not a hot dog!
jokes.setup: Joey shares his precious sandwich with Chandler
jokes.punchline: Warning him to eat it carefully because it's special
jokes.explanation: Good payoff - Joey shares his most prized possession. Character growth moment.

jokes.text: You're showing me how much I mean to you. / With a bite. / Jeez!
jokes.setup: Joey said sharing sandwich shows how much Chandler means to him
jokes.punchline: Chandler taking an enormous bite, Joey's horror
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Chandler's big bite vs Joey's protective instincts.

jokes.text: You are in our apartment all the time! This is just a drop in the bucket, mister!
jokes.setup: Rachel questions why Ross is at their apartment
jokes.punchline: Ross's defensive justification about reciprocal visits
jokes.explanation: Ross's defensive explanation. Decent character moment showing his justification.

jokes.text: The most amazing thing happened tonight. I thought my number was up. I had an actual near-death experience. / What happened? / We were on the ride-along with Gary and somebody took a shot at me! / Really? / No. A car backfired.
jokes.setup: Ross dramatically describes near-death experience
jokes.punchline: Build-up to shooting that was just a car backfire
jokes.explanation: Good build-up and deflation. Ross's dramatic retelling vs mundane reality.

jokes.text: That was not a near-death experience. That was barely an experience. / You weren't there!
jokes.setup: Rachel dismisses Ross's dramatic claims
jokes.punchline: Ross's defensive 'you weren't there' response
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Rachel's dismissal vs Ross's defensive position. 'Barely an experience' is a great line.

jokes.text: Hey, was Monica here? / I want my money back. / You probably need that for stamps, right?
jokes.setup: Ross asks about Monica visiting
jokes.punchline: Rachel references his message about getting stamps
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the stamps line from earlier. Good economy.

jokes.text: I wonder how I'd react under fire. And not backfire... but heavy fire. Like, if I was in a war. Man, I would be great in a war! I think I'd make a fantastic military leader. I mean, I know I'd make general before any of you guys.
jokes.setup: Joey reflecting on his heroic moment
jokes.punchline: His escalating fantasy about being a great military leader
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Joey's ego inflating from one heroic act to military fantasy.

jokes.text: Before or after you were shot by your own troops?
jokes.setup: Joey claiming he'd make general quickly
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggesting Joey would be so bad his own troops would shoot him
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler comeback - dark military humor with perfect timing. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I know Joey would be in the foxhole protecting all of us. / If the foxhole was lined with sandwiches.
jokes.setup: Ross praises Joey's protective nature
jokes.punchline: Chandler adds condition that foxhole would need sandwiches
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Joey's food priorities. Great setup/punchline structure.

jokes.text: Yeah, hero sandwiches.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Joey and sandwiches in foxholes
jokes.punchline: Joey's pun on 'hero sandwiches' relating to his heroic moment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - ties together Joey's heroic moment and food obsession perfectly. Great economy.

jokes.text: You all know I'm a pacifist, so I'm not interested in war in any way. But when the revolution comes, I'll have to destroy you all. Not you, Joey.
jokes.setup: Phoebe declares herself a pacifist
jokes.punchline: Immediately talks about destroying everyone in a revolution, except Joey
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - contradictory logic delivered with complete sincerity. The Joey exception adds another layer.

tmdb_overview: The group joins Gary on a ride-along; Rachel accidentally deletes Emily's answering machine message for Ross.
